mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes, rising CPIAUCSL forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations that compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative predictive relationship reverses or vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, or 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which directly correlates with higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show no directional bias during industrial expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll decline exceeding 50,000 signals abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50000 in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of instances following a >50k payroll drop, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with stable inflation creates a soft landing environment that supports sustained equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) or fails to predict positive returns when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% and CPIAUCSL remains below 3% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle regimes, declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment confirms demand destruction, forcing equity repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative signal fails to materialize when INDPRO falls and UNRATE rises simultaneously, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of late-cycle contraction observations.
